objects.name: MacSync Stealer
objects.description: MacSync Stealer is a macOS infostealer malware that has evolved from drag-to-terminal and ClickFix-style techniques to a sophisticated code-signed and notarized Swift application. It is delivered via disk images containing decoy files and executes obfuscated bash scripts retrieved from remote servers, primarily for credential and information theft.

objects.name: Odyssey Infostealer
objects.description: Odyssey Infostealer is a macOS infostealer malware observed to adopt similar distribution methods as MacSync Stealer, including delivery via code-signed and notarized disk images.

objects.name: zk-call-messenger-installer-3.9.2-lts.dmg
objects.description: Disk image containing the code signed and notarized executable for MacSync Stealer.

objects.name: co.runtime.helper.b3f9a2.dmg
objects.description: Disk image containing the code signed and notarized executable.

objects.name: runner
objects.description: Initial obfuscated bash script that gets downloaded into /tmp/runner and executed as part of MacSync Stealer infection chain.

objects.name: GNJLS3UYZ4
objects.description: Apple Developer Team ID used to code sign and notarize MacSync Stealer executables. Certificate was revoked after reporting.

objects.name: Code-Signed and Notarized Malware Delivery
objects.description: Malware is delivered as a code-signed and notarized Swift application within a disk image, leveraging Apple's developer infrastructure to evade detection and appear legitimate.

objects.name: Obfuscated Bash Script Execution
objects.description: Malware uses obfuscated bash scripts, often encoded in base64 and decompressed with gunzip, to execute payloads in memory with minimal disk trace.

objects.name: Second-Stage Payload Retrieval via Curl
objects.description: Malware fetches second-stage payloads using curl commands with custom flags and user agent strings, often from remote endpoints.

objects.name: Gatekeeper Evasion
objects.description: Malware clears the com.apple.quarantine extended attribute and validates downloaded files to evade Apple's Gatekeeper security mechanisms.

objects.name: Disk Image Size Inflation
objects.description: Malware disk images are inflated in size by embedding large decoy files, such as PDFs, to appear more legitimate or evade detection.

objects.name: Rate Limiting Execution
objects.description: Malware enforces rate limiting by storing timestamps and deferring execution if a minimum interval has not passed, reducing detection likelihood.

objects.name: Network Environment Checks
objects.description: Malware checks for internet connectivity before proceeding with payload retrieval and execution, avoiding execution in offline or sandboxed environments.

objects.name: Persistence via Application Support and Log Files
objects.description: Malware creates directories and files in ~/Library/Application Support/UserSyncWorker/ and ~/Library/Logs/UserSyncWorker.log to track execution and maintain persistence.

objects.name: Drag-to-Terminal and ClickFix Social Engineering
objects.description: Earlier variants of MacSync Stealer used drag-to-terminal and ClickFix techniques to trick users into executing base64 encoded commands.

objects.name: Use of osascript Dialogs
objects.description: After payload execution, malware displays osascript dialogs as part of its infection chain.

objects.name: MacSync Stealer
objects.description: Swift-based, code-signed and notarized macOS stealer malware.

objects.name: Odyssey infostealer
objects.description: macOS infostealer malware using similar distribution methods.
